reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All three FP gates passed: (1) Code is actively used in P2P gossip validation and sync paths, not dead code. (2) Attack path is via untrusted P2P network (data_column gossip), not dependent on compromised/faulty EL. (3) PeerDAS (EIP-7594) is explicitly in-scope. However, this is a single-client bug affecting only Prysm. Severity downgraded from Critical to Medium because Prysm's 31% network share is below the 33% threshold required for High severity (per severity_classification in BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json). An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by sending a valid DataColumnSidecar A, then sending forged DataColumnSidecar M with same inclusion proof and header but malicious KzgCommitments, causing cache collision that bypasses full Merkle verification.
